questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: The magnetic field $\quad \vec{B}=2 \times 10^{-5} \sin \left\{\pi\left(0.5 \times 10^3 x+1.5 \times 10^{11} t\right)\right\} \hat{j} T \quad$ represents a plane electromagnetic wave travelling in space with $x$ in meter and t in second. The correct statement(s) are
options: The wave length of the wave is 4.0 mm and its frequency is 75 GHz
The energy density associated with the wave is nearly $=316 \mu J / \mathrm{m}^3$
The electric field vector is $\vec{E}=-6000 \sin \left[\pi\left(0.5 \times 10^3 x-1.5 \times 10^{11} t\right)\right] \hat{k} \mathrm{Vm}^{-1}$
The electric field vector is $\vec{E}=6000 \sin \left[\pi\left(0.5 \times 10^3 x+1.5 \times 10^{11} t\right)\right] \hat{k} \mathrm{Vm}^{-1}$

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: The electric field component of an electromagnetic wave is expressed as $E=(3 j+b k) \times 10^{-3} \sin \left[10^7(x+2 y+3 z-\beta t)\right]$ in SI units. Taking $c=3 \times 10^8 m \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$ as the speed of electromagnetic wave in vacuum, choose the correct option(s)
options: The value of constant beta is $\beta=3 \times 10^8 \times \sqrt{14}$
The value of constant b is $\mathrm{b}=2$.
The average energy density of the em wave is $U=6.5 \times 10^{-6} \varepsilon_0$ in SI units.
The amplitude of magnetic field is $B=1.20 \times 10^{-11}$ Tesla
